response: {"purpose":"This figure demonstrates that normalizing eCLIP data against a paired Size-Matched Input (SMInput) library substantially improves the ability to distinguish true protein–RNA binding sites from background noise. Using SLBP and RBFOX2 as case studies, the figure shows that SMInput normalization elevates biologically meaningful clusters (e.g., those overlapping histone RNAs or RBFOX2-dependent cassette exon flanking introns) in ranked significance lists compared to pre-normalized CLIPper scores. Additionally, cross-replicate fold-enrichment comparisons and Irreproducible Discovery Rate (IDR) analyses confirm that SMInput normalization yields highly reproducible binding site identification between biological replicates while clearly separating true signal from IgG controls. These results matter because they establish SMInput normalization as a critical analytical step for achieving both specificity and reproducibility in eCLIP datasets.","title":"SMInput Normalization Improves eCLIP Signal, Specificity, and Reproducibility"}
